svg.text: Your Guide to High Blood Pressure
Navigation is always present, but the content cadence changes as you move through the page.
Your Guide to High Blood Pressure
One authoritative page that explains the numbers, the risk, the urgency, the body impact,
and the daily plan without forcing people through a chain of signpost pages.
Use the live board direction: adult vector figures, warm bone fields,
shallow depth, restrained red focal cue, and an instructional scene.
Chosen live reference: calm home blood-pressure reading, not lifestyle photography.
This page should read like a real guide.
Show useful summary text immediately, not just
- there is a clear urgent threshold
- lifestyle and medication can both be valid
This is still the primary teaching module, but it now sits inside a denser context with
more explanation, more quick help, and clearer expansion points.
Make this big enough that users can see the action without leaving the page.
Add more visible explanatory text here:
what systolic means, what diastolic means, why one reading is not the
whole story, and how home readings should be logged.
What to bring to a visit
Keep the main message plain: most people will not feel obvious symptoms, so this chapter
should feel clear and stabilising, not alarmist.
Risk factor chips and audience-specific paths
That is why this page needs more visible
readings and action guidance than today.
instead of hiding it behind another page.
How high blood pressure affects the body
This becomes a more editorial band. Not a grid of pages, not a list of links, but one
clear body story with optional expansion points.
Body illustration anchor with callouts around it.
Short summary first. Deep dive link stays optional.
Keep the consequence visible in context instead of sending users away immediately.
Explain monitoring and when to ask for more evaluation.
Severity stays real, but the tone stays calm and useful.
This is where the page needs to be content-rich. The current architecture jumps here the most.
In the new model, this becomes a deep chapter with tabs, summaries, and inline expansions.
A realistic starter plan, not a list of route-outs
Visible summary text gives returning users something to learn even before they expand deeper panels.
Start with a reading habit, reduce one obvious sodium source, build one realistic activity routine,
and decide with your clinician whether medication should be part of the plan.
Expand: stress, sleep, and habit barriers
Expand: what to ask your care team
This chapter is intentionally denser and deeper than the others because management is where
Diet and fitness ecosystem pages can still exist, but they should not be required for the
This is the bridge between one-page authority and expandable depth. Each chapter carries a short
summary, an open/close state, and optional specialist links.
What the reading means, when it is urgent, and how to track it.
Chapter 2: Lower it and manage it
Daily actions, medication context, and care-team coordination.
Heart, stroke, kidneys, vision, and what changes the risk.
Black adults, children and teens, pulmonary hypertension, low BP.
These stay on the page as expandable modules first. Only the true adjacent conditions stay as
Tools belong inside the guide so the user can act without being thrown into a separate branch.
Make the ending useful. Keep recurrent anxieties together instead of scattering them across smaller leaves.
Can I feel high blood pressure?
How often should I check it at home?
Authority is visible at both the top and bottom of the page, so the page feels reviewed, current,
and clinically responsible the whole way through.
measure it, track it, and make a plan.
V2 intent: no hero photography, illustration-led authority, visible review metadata, denser content, stable nav, varied section rhythm.
